lines: "You told Eleazar the truth, which takes either bravery or cruelty. Often the same boot, different mud."
"He always did believe a hard answer could be polished into sense. I hope he keeps one corner of himself unpolished."
"Eleazar is smiling. I can hear it from here when the wind turns stupid."
"If you lied, I will not ask you to confess. This road already collects enough little crimes."
"So you found Eleazar. If he called himself a field prince, forgive him; wheat gives a man ideas."
"The Choir promised him rapture, crowns, a world full of kneeling. They promised me the same. I asked who would scratch my nose after the coronation. No answer yet."

choices.label: Ask if he trusts the Choir

lines: "The wheat, friend. If Eleazar starts talking crowns, listen for the fear under it."

choices.label: Ask if he trusts the Choir

lines: "Back again. Either the road turned you around, or you like difficult conversation."
"I can offer directions, insults, and one request I have not managed to walk anywhere myself."

choices.label: Ask if he trusts the Choir

lines: The calcified man stands at the road fork with both arms fixed into directions. One hand points up the Hallowfen road. The other points toward the farm, though two fingers have been snapped off and tied back on with wire.
Names, luck marks, and worse words have been scratched across his pale ribs. Pebbles, old spit, and a little pile of ducats sit at his feet.
"Well? Nothing? Not even a spit in the face? Standards have fallen since the Stilling."

choices.label: I am not spitting on you

effects.log: Gideon takes the insult like weather.

lines: "A reformer, after only a century and change. I knew one would come."
"Name is Gideon Carbo. One hand points to Hallowfen, the other to the farm. The road kept the rest."

choices.label: Ask why he points to the farm
Ask if he trusts the Choir

lines: "The Stilling caught me with both arms out during an argument. They set in opposite directions, and I have not bent a finger since."

"Eleazar called it useful. The first Carbo sons after us hauled me to this fork because the pose fit both roads. They planted me with one hand toward Hallowfen and the other toward the farm; then the caravans learned their signpost could talk."
"There it is: good lungs wasted on weak vocabulary."
"A trader once wrote half a sermon on my hip and charged passersby to read it. I remember the knife more than the words."

effects.log: Gideon laughs once. It sounds like a hinge full of grit.

lines: "Careful. There are only so many clean places left on me for new words."
"Now do a useful cruelty and walk to the wheat. My brother is out there being converted by compliments."
"Children threw mud first; adults improved it with theology."
"Pilgrims pay in ducats or spit, then ask which road is safest. Some carve their initials into my stomach when honesty displeases them."
"The Choir saw the stone in my ribs and promoted me to holy royalty. When I asked whether kings still get rained on, they promised rapture instead."
"When they asked the way to the Carbo house, they followed the arm already fixed toward the farm. Nobody in red cloth came back."
"Rapture has been coming for one hundred years. The rain reaches me more often."

choices.label: Ask what he needs from you

lines: "I do not believe them. Road priests and gutter saints have promised me release before, usually while reaching for the coins."
"The Choir is better dressed. That is not a doctrine."

choices.label: Ask what he needs from you

lines: "Maybe. I am a road sign made of infected stone. I keep a small purse open for surprises."
"But every prophet who says I will rule the world has still asked me which way to Hallowfen. A king notices that."

choices.label: Ask what he needs from you

lines: "The Choir came through here calling me holy because the Host left me standing and talking. They asked for the Carbo house, the one our brother's line built around us."
"My joints have not moved since the Stilling. They followed the hand fixed toward the farm, went on to the yard, and never came back."
"Eleazar is in the wheat east of the farm, drinking every compliment they spilled on the way."
"Check on him before their hymns get too deep in him."

effects.log: Gideon tells you to take the luck money at his feet.

lines: You give the Censure read aloud: speech intact, no Host drive in the voice. Stage IV.
"Is that the merciful number?" Gideon asks.
You say it is the number that means Eleazar is still Eleazar.
"Then hurry before the Choir teaches him a holier one."

effects.log: Gideon tells you to take the luck money at his feet.

lines: "Take the ducats that pilgrims threw for luck; luck and I are not on speaking terms."
"Tell Eleazar his brother still points. He will think that is noble. Do not correct him too fast."
"The wheat, friend. If Eleazar starts talking crowns, listen for the fear under it."
"Luck money. Pilgrims toss it, then ask which hand marks the blessed city. I name the hand, and they thank me by missing my feet half the time."
"If a coin hits my face, I try not to flinch."

choices.label: Ask what he needs from you
